races.analysis: Cathedral (GB) towers on class here — her 118 life-best from the Breeders' Cup Filly & Mare Turf is 8 points clear of anything else in the field, and the engine makes her a ~54% standout even off the 8½-month layoff with no published US works. Dynamic Pricing (IRE), a two-time Saratoga winner whose Beaugay (102) and 2025 form fits the inner-turf route profile, is the clear second-tier play ahead of the Chad Brown trio's depth.

Sapphire Beach (IRE) pairs the race's best recent figures (102 and a 109 ceiling this spring) with a presser style in a Mellon-turf sprint where E/P types own the top win share, and the engine gives her ~46%. Cy Fair owns the tallest single number — the 118 from the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint — but her 2026 pair (108-112 with a 5-wide first call) leaves her the logical exacta partner rather than the top pick.
